rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of This Courts May 16, 2019 Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 13, 2019, Line 1, ALESCO PREFERRED FUNDING VIII, LTD., ALESCO PREFERRED FUNDING XI, LTD., ALESCO PREFERRED FUNDING XII, LTD., ALESCO PREFERRED FUNDING XIV, LTD., HILDENE OPPORTUNTIES MASTER FUND II, LTD., NFC PARTNERS, LLC, WOLF RIVER OPPORTUNITY FUND LLC, WOLF RIVER PARTNER FUND, AND WT HOLDINGS, INC.'S Motion For Reconsideration Of This Courts May 16, 2019 Order. Movants' motion for reconsideration of the Court's May 16, 2019 order is denied. Movants have not shown any "new or different facts, circumstances, or law" warranting reconsideration of the Court's order. (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 1008(a).) Nor is the Court inclined to grant reconsideration of the order on its own motion. (See Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal.4th 249.) The Court made it clear at the very outset of the hearing that it was inclined to give "substantial weight" (but not dispositive weight) to the Insurance Commissioner's views. (RT (Mar. 11, 2019) at 3:25-4:8.) Movants appeared to agree, since in their moving papers they asserted (overly optimistically, as it turned out) that the Commissioner supported their position. (See Mot. at 1:19-21 ["Movants understand that the Insurance Commissioner agrees with their view."].) In any event, Movants had ample opportunity, both at the hearing and in their post-hearing submission, to take issue with the Court's views on that subject. Further, while it is true that the Commissioner changed his position to some degree following the hearing, Movants consented to the procedure proposed by the Court for the parties to submit simultaneous post-hearing briefs and proposed orders. If Movants were taken by surprise by the Commissioner's position, they could have sought leave to file a further brief before the Court issued its order more than 60 days later. Finally, Movants devote the bulk of their motion not to the question whether the Commissioner's position is entitled to deference, but rather to rearguing issues and authority that were raised in the prior briefing, hearing, and post-hearing submissions, or could have been. As such, reconsideration is not appropriate. (See New York Times Co. v. Superior Court (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 206, 212 [moving party must provide satisfactory explanation for the failure to make the showing of new or different facts, circumstances, or law at or before the time the challenged order was issued]; Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500 [reconsideration not warranted based on claim that trial court misinterpreted applicable law in its initial decision].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the interested parties ACP Re, Ltd., et al., is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Insurance Commissioners Notice Of Application And Application For Order Approving Financial Report, Expenses Of Administration, And Estate Administration Matters; Financial Report On Status Of Castlepoint National Insurance Company In Liquidation; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 13, 2019, Line 1, Insurance Commissioners' Application For Order Approving Financial Report, Expenses Of Administration, And Estate Administration Matters; Financial Report On Status Of Castlepoint National Insurance Company In Liquidation. Hearing required. While no opposition to the application has been filed, the Commissioner should be prepared to address the following questions, among others: 1. Will the proposed records retention and destruction plan ensure that any records relevant to pending contested proofs of claim, such as those of Eita Pruss and of the TruPS holders, are preserved? 2. How does the Commissioner propose to address his suspicion that "a number" of the $15 million in outstanding checks were actually cashed by recipients prior to conservation but were not voided and appropriately accounted for in the system by CastlePoint? 3. What is the aggregate face amount of the 10,499 outstanding checks to policyholders that the Commissioner proposes to classify as de minimis Class 2 claims for which no proof of claim forms would be mailed? How many proofs of claims for less than $100 have been received in response to earlier mailings? (Holloway Decl. Para. 26.) Is there an alternative, less costly approach to sending further mailings and having the CLO process such claims, such as providing policyholders a link to a website containing a proof of claim form and information regarding how to complete and file it? =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE APPLICATION OF: DELOITTE TOUCHE LLP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Enforce Subpoena For Production Of Documents Pursuant To Ccp 2029.600
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 13, 2019, Line 2, PETITIONER DELOITTE TOUCHE LLP'S Petition To Enforce Subpoena For Production Of Documents Pursuant To CCP 2029.600. The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted in its entirety. Petitioner Deloitte & Touche LLP's petition to enforce subpoena for production of documents is granted. Respondent GPS Capital Management, LLC shall produce, no later than August 31, 2019, all non-privileged documents responsive to categories 3, 7, 8, 9, and 15 of the deposition subpoena duces tecum issued by Deloitte, and shall concurrently serve a privilege log with sufficient factual information to enable Deloitte to evaluate the merits of each claim of privilege or protected work product asserted by GPS with respect to any documents withheld on those grounds. GPS shall bear its attorney's fees in reviewing the documents for privilege in response to Deloitte's subpoena. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Deloitte is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and attaches the report and recommendation of the judge pro tem and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES R. SCHWAB JR VS. ROYSE LAW FIRM, P.C. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 13, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF CHARLES SCHWAB'S Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs. Plaintiff Charles R. Schwab Jr.'s motion to strike or tax costs is granted in part, and defendants Royse Law Firm, P.C. and Roger Royse are awarded $8,991.65 in costs. Defendants are entitled to recover the following statutory costs, in the total amount of $8,991.65: -Filing and motion fees ($1,540.00) (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 1033.5(a)(1)); -Jury fees ($330.00) (Sec. 1033.5(a)(1)); -Expenses of taking, video recording, and transcribing necessary depositions ($6,138.60) (Sec. 1033.5(a)(3)). Plaintiff, having failed to prevail in this case on summary judgment, cannot be heard after the fact to complain that depositions that Defendants took were not "necessary." (See Nelson v. Anderson (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 111, 132 [need for deposition is determined from the pretrial vantage point of a litigant; challenging party bears the burden the prove the costs unnecessary at the time of incurring it or unreasonable].) -Service of process ($983.05) (Sec. 1033.5(a)(4)). Because defendants did not file a declaration in opposition to the motion to tax or strike, they have not met their burden to show that the nonstatutory costs claimed in the memorandum of costs were reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation or reasonable in amount. (Sec. 1033.5(c)(2),(4); see Nelson v. Anderson, 72 Cal.App.4th at 132.) Accordingly, the motion to strike those costs is granted. rulings.Case Title: CHARLES SCHWAB JR. VS. BRIAN BOGOSIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Court Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 13, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT BRIAN BOGOSIAN'S Motion To Seal Court Records. Grant. No opposition filed, and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which sets forth the express findings required by Cal. R. Ct. 2.550(d) and specifies the exhibits to be sealed, and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY S. FRUCIANO VS. IRA D. SHARLIP M.D ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 13, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT IRA SHARLIP'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Ira Sharlip, M.D.'s demurrer to the cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained without leave to amend. The court will hear this demurrer even though defendant failed to provide sufficient notice in accordance with Code of Civ. Proc., Sec. 1005. This demurer was originally filed in December 2018 and was fully briefed before the court took it off calendar in May 2019. Since all of the parties have already addressed the merits of the demurrer, there is no point in taking it off calendar again. (See Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697 ["It is well settled that the appearance of a party at the hearing of a motion and his or her opposition to the motion on its merits is a waiver of any defects or irregularities in the notice of motion. This rule applies even when no notice was given at all."].) "Whether a defendant's conduct can reasonably be found to be outrageous is a question of law that must initially be determined by the court; if reasonable persons may differ, it is for the jury to determine whether the conduct was, in fact, outrageous. [citation] Ordinarily, a medical diagnosis and treatment advice will not be considered outrageous unless they are false and given in bad faith." (Berkley v. Dowds (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 518, 533-534 [affirming order sustaining demurrer to intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action where defendant physician urged plaintiff to terminate husband's life support to reduce costs].) In this case, plaintiff fails to plead outrageous conduct as a matter of law. "Liability has been found only where the conduct has been so outrageous in character, and so extreme in degree, as to go beyond all possible bounds of decency, and to be regarded as atrocious, and utterly intolerable in a civilized community." (Cochran v. Cochran (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 488, 496.) Plaintiff's complaint does not allege such intolerable conduct and rude, callous or unprofessional conduct cannot support the claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
